bind.title: Federation bind
bind.readonlyHint: Object is bound to a remote peer (read-only bind metadata).
bind.subtitle: Local path {{path}} is canonical. Remote peer is the source of data and behavior.
bind.mirrorCallout: Synchronized catalog — use "Place locally" to create a local bind.
peers.column.name: Name
peers.newPeerHint: Loopback (same ISPF): leave token empty — sync uses your Bearer token. For a remote peer with RBAC, specify a service account token.
peers.field.name: Name
peers.getAuthTokenHint: Loopback: "Current session token". Remote peer: login via server (password is not stored).
peers.errorOutboundFields: Specify name, hub URL and registration code
peers.errorSecretsKeyLength: Key must be at least 16 characters
catalogSync.title: Sync catalog — {{peer}}
peers.remoteTokenReceivedExpiry: Token received from remote peer ({{username}}), expires {{expiresAt}}
tokens.title: Federation token (this node)
tokens.subtitle: Issues a Bearer session for the selected user (default TTL 12 h). Use on another ISPF as the auth token for a peer pointing at this instance.
tunnel.inboundHint: Issue a one-time registration code for an edge agent behind NAT. The code is shown once.
tunnel.field.name: Name
tunnel.column.name: Name
tunnel.outboundHint: Outbound WebSocket tunnel to public hub. Edge needs an encryption key to store registration code and session token (ispf.security.secrets-key).
tunnel.secretsKeyYaml: — set in application.yml or ISPF_SECURITY_SECRETS_KEY env var
tunnel.secretsKeyMissing: Key not configured. Set it below or configure ispf.security.secrets-key in server config and restart ISPF.
tunnel.secretsKeySaveHint: Store the key in a safe place. Changing the key will break existing outbound agents.
tunnel.secretsKeyChangeHint: To change the key, delete all outbound agents and save a new encryption key.
tunnel.secretsKeySavedEdge: Encryption key saved on edge. You can add an outbound agent.
tunnel.activeSessions: Active tunnel sessions on this hub: {{count}}
probe.title: Proxy read probe
probe.subtitle: Performs GET object via selected federation peer to verify connectivity and authorization.
panel.title: Federation
panel.subtitle: Registry of remote ISPF instances. Object path and service endpoint are separate: for loopback leave the token empty; for a remote peer specify a service account token or issue a token on the Tokens tab.
panel.adminOnly: Federation is available to administrators only.
panel.tokenApiMissing: Backend without federation token API (requires capabilities federation-issue-token and federation-remote-token). Rebuild and restart ispf-server, then refresh the page.
